RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.6.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Command line option '--dll_include' can be used to specify locations to search for custom code generator DLLs.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.6.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The Ioc.h file will include Os.h automatically if it has not already been included.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.6.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Clarify that Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess is only called in Scalability Classes 3 and 4. Non-trusted code can be configured in any Scalability class.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.6.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Enhanced checking of OS APIs that are allowed in cross-core IOC callbacks. Only APIs that are legal from an idle context are allowed when Service Protection is enabled.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.6.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The ETAS Licensing Manager installer is no longer packaged with the RTA-OS installer. It can be downloaded separately from the ETAS website.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incompatible target options in ARXML
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When migrating a configuration from one target type to another, it was possible for target options from the original target to be partly interpreted by the new target and cause unexpected output. The OS now rejects target options that are not applicable to the new target.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Memory sections are not generated fully in Os_BSWMD when using custom DLL
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When a customer was using their Custom DLL code generator, not all of the newly created MemMap sections get reflected into the OS BSWMD file.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Leaving Tasks/ISRs without releasing a spinlock might prevent further spinlocks from being taken, resulting in an error notification and invalid behavior.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS now correctly cleans up unreleased spinlocks with predecessors when a TASK or ISR terminates without explicitly releasing them.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.4.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adds support for AUTOSAR 4.9.0 (R23-11).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.4.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Data in grids in the RTA-OS GUI will now sort numerically when the contents are integers.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.4.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The RTA-OS tools have been updated to use .NET8 instead of .NET6 internally.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.4.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The RTAOSGen GUI no longer leaves a console window open when it starts.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.4.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Integer values in the incoming XML can use the full unsigned 64-bit range. Checks are made that values do not exceed the capability of relevant compilation types.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.4.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The documentation is updated to remove StartupHook as a callable context for StartScheduleTableAbs, StartScheduleTableRel, SetAbsAlarm, SetRelAlarm, SetScheduleTableAsync, StartScheduleTableSynchron and SyncScheduleTable.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.4.0/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The documentation for the 'Optimize for core-local memory' option is updated to explain that this is a performance improvement but it is still necessary for the data to be globally accessible by other cores.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible build fail in IOC generation for receivers without IDs
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If an IOC communication had multiple receivers without separate IDs, the build could fail if some but not all contained a receiver callback.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: IOC cross-core ReceiverPullCB potentially use a wrong trusted function index when --ioc:impl is used.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: This could occur when some communications with Rx callbacks were single-core and others cross-core

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.3.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If an IOC Receive callback belongs to the same OS Application as the sender, its invocation will be optimized to a direct function call.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.3.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: BSWMD content is modified so that it contains IOC sections even when they have no run-time content.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.3.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Command line option '--ar_version:?' can be used to list the supported AUTOSAR versions.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.3.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A new SPDX format SBOM report has been added that describes the tools and target version that was used to generate the RTA-OS code.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.3.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When the OS option 'IOC Code' is set to 'CallerApp', memory sections used for IOC APIs default to the form ..._SEC_CODE_{app}.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.3.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The MAKW used for RTE IOC Rx callbacks matches that used by the TrustedFunction that calls it. Previously, unless a MemoryMappingCodeLocationRef was specified, it could differ.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: The --samples command-line option only worked with the overwrite modifier.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The code tried to emit the Os_MemMap.h sample file twice. The tool would complain about the second save unless overwrite was specified.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation error in TerminateApplication.c
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: It was possible to see a compile error saying that a function was declared but never referenced in TerminateApplication.c. This would only appear in configurations where there were shared TASK priorities, TerminateApplication was allowed and the OS-Applications which could be terminated did not use shared priorities.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible compile fail with OS option "Always clear OS data" and IOC.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: With OS Option 'Always clear OS data' set to TRUE, it was possible for some of the declarations for IOC data to get omitted from the output files. This would result in a compile failure.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adds support for AUTOSAR 4.8.0 (R22-11).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Linux versions of the command-line tools are packaged in the RTA-OS installer.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MemoryMappingCodeLocationRef is supported on Alarm callbacks.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: New OS option 'IOC Spinlocks' is added to control how the IOC allocates its cross-core spinlocks.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Unless configured with a MemoryMappingCodeLocationRef element, TASKs are put in the memory section CODE_(TaskName).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Unless configured with a MemoryMappingCodeLocationRef element, Category 2 ISRs are put in the memory section CODE_(IsrName).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When the OS option 'IOC Code' is not set to 'Caller', memory sections used for IOC APIs follow the specification for R21-11 (OS_(IE)_START_SEC_CODE).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: All MAKW defines used for the MemMap mechanism are forced to be upper case.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Duplicate entries in the sample header file 'Os_MemMap.h' have been removed.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/10
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: IOC Rx callbacks are placed in the memory section of the relevant OS Application.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/11
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: IOC inline macros add parenthesis around the value parameter.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/12
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: StartNonAutosarCore is documented as being optional. It is removed in the AUTOSAR R22-11 specification, but remains in the RTA-OS code for backwards compatibility.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/13
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The sample header file 'Platform_Types.h' modifies the definition of Boolean when it recognizes that the compiler is C99 compliant.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/14
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS GUI Project Properties dialog now correctly reports the AUTOSAR version of projects that have been read in.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/15
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS GUI copes properly with screen size changes.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/16
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The Schedule Table Assistant in the OS GUI preserves all other Schedule Table settings when regenerating expiry points.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/17
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The tools now accept malformed input ARXML where target options are missing a VALUE element.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/18
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: GetApplicationState, TryToGetSpinlock, ReadPeripheral8, ReadPeripheral16 and ReadPeripheral32 now return E_OS_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS if an out parameter references an invalid address (EXTENDED mode).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/19
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fix for PPCe200 targets allow them to compile correctly in systems with over 31 TASK events.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/20
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fix for some PPCe200 targets that might fail to compile unless ORTI was enabled.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.2.0/21
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fix for possible compilation error on some targets when using more than 32 OS-Applications.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: TerminateApplication does not fully terminate a task if it has multiple activations.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If a task had multiple queued activations, TerminateApplication would only terminate one instance of the task and any queued activations would still run.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: REENTRANCY-LEVEL element in BSWMD should only appear for some AUTOSAR version.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The BSWMD report emitted a REENTRANCY-LEVEL element for all AUTOSAR versions. It now only emits it for versions 4.1.1 onwards.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: A spurious resource configuration error could be reported.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: This only happened on systems with a single OS-Application and resources on a non-master core.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.1.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adds support for AUTOSAR 4.7.0 (R21-11).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.1.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MemoryMappingCodeLocationRef is supported on OS-Application. This provides a default MemMap code location for Hooks, TrustedFunctions, Tasks and ISRs that belong to the OS-Application.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.1.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MemoryMappingCodeLocationRef is supported on TrustedFunction (not editable in RTAOSCfg). This means that trusted functions can be located in individually configured MemMap sections.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.1.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The IOC command line option --ioc:impl can be given an optional namespace by using the form --ioc:[ns]impl.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.1.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Minor documentation updates.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible imbalance of MemMap START_SEC/STOP_SEC in Os_Cfg.c.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A change was added in 6.2.200 that means that it is possible for an OS_START_SEC_CONST_UNSPECIFIED to get omitted in Os_Cfg.c. This only happens in systems with no OS Resources, so a workaround is to create a dummy resource.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: CallTrustedFunction returns E_OS_ACCESS in STANDARD status.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The RTA-OS documentation says that E_OS_ACCESS should only be emitted in EXTENDED status, matching the AUTOSAR specification. The OS code no longer checks for E_OS_ACCESS in STANDARD status.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: RTA-OS 12.0.0 GUI does not open reports.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The Reports and Help-Documentation menu items in RTAOSCfg do not display the relevant documents. Reports do get generated on disk, but they do not get shown.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: RTA-OS 12.0.0 GUI does not start correctly on some PCs.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The GUI might fault during startup on non-English locale PCs. The workaround is to switch temporarily to an English locale.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: IOC reads for communications with ReceiverIDs might omit a read spinlock.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In a multicore IOC system, if the receivers were using ReceiverIDs then the OS was treating the read as for single core. It then omitted a read spinlock on non-atomic communications, so a reader might read while the writer was updating the same data.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.0.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS tools are based on .NET 6 technology. This replaces the .NET framework technology used previously. Do not install this version over the top of earlier versions. The names of the command-line and GUI tools have been modified to RTAOSGen.exe and RTAOSCfg.exe.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.0.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The Reference Guide now lists those OS APIs that can be called from Os_Cbk_Idle().

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.0.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The MemMap section for Os_const_tasks0 has been adjusted to add _LOCAL in the single-core case for relevant AUTOSAR versions.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.0.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: An error is raised if --ioc:impl and --ioc:stub are used at the same time.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.0.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The IOC initialization is optimized to use the memory clear function Os_ioc_memclr instead of individual assignments (may depend on target). Os_ioc_memclr has the same features as Os_ioc_memcpy.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.0.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: There is a new 'Release Note' report that can be used to generate a machine-readable release note that contains information from both the OS Tools and the Target Port.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.0.0/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When the OS option 'IOC Atomic Copy Size' is active, the OS is able to take input from 2 optional files. The files should contain the names of IOC communications, one per line. Any communication in the ioc_atomic_exclude.txt file is excluded from the optimization. Any communication in the ioc_atomic_include.txt file is included in the optimization (regardless of its size). RTA-OS looks for the files in the 'project' directory - which is likely to be the directory containing the first .arxml or .rtaos file passed to RTAOSGen.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 12.0.0/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The IOC Summary Report has been updated to show the reason for inlining optimizations.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible compilation failure using --ioc:impl option.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In cases where the IOC used trusted functions to implement callbacks, there might be a compile error in --ioc:impl mode if the configuration specified that these functions should be implemented in the memory section of the owning OS Application.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Too many cross-core trusted function calls in --ioc:impl mode.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In cases where the IOC uses trusted functions to implement cross-core callbacks, the cross-core check code might attempt to run functions on the wrong cores (in addition to running them on the correct ones). The faulty calls can be expected to be rejected (E_OS_ACCESS) but they will waste execution time.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: RTA-OS BSWMD Report fails with --ioc:impl mode.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The RTA-OS tool would fail if the BSWMD report was requested in a build where --ioc:impl mode was also selected.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Task activation count was limited to 255.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The number of activations per task was limited to 255, but is now 4294967295.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.200/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Remove the possible double reporting of the error message 'Status must be EXTENDED in SC3 or SC4'

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.200/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: IOC internal data declarations emitted are in size order - largest first - to try to minimize fragmentation

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.200/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: TrustedFunctions are declared in the MemMap section of the owning OS App

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.200/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Range checking for peripheral access APIs considers both start and end range

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.200/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Os_PeripheralAddressType size fixed at 'large integer' size

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.200/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Core-specific task constants are placed in per-code memory sections.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.200/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Corrected documentation example for Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.200/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Documentation for GetElapsedValue shows that it can return E_OS_VALUE.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.200/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Documentation for GetActiveApplicationMode states that it may be called before StartOS().

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation fails on targets that support Enhanced Isolation without selecting full Memory Protection.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS now forces full memory protection to be used whenever Enhanced Isolation is selected.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Multi-core initialization to INVALID_OSAPPLICATION with AUTOSAR version 4.5+.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: It was possible for one of the OS-internal variables to skip initialization to INVALID_OSAPPLICATION in some multi-core configurations. This was only an issue when the INVALID_OSAPPLICATION value was non-zero, as specified for AUTOSAR 4.5+. For a workaround the OS option 'ApplicationType starts at zero' could be set to FALSE.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: IOC compilation failure with array types smaller than "IOC Atomic Copy Size"
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Array types are now excluded from the "IOC Atomic Copy Size" optimization. The C language does not suppport array assignment copies.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible compilation failure connected to OS-Application Error Hooks
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: On some targets using stack realignment and per OS-Application Error Hooks, a compilation error might occur in configurations where not all untrusted or Trusted-With-Protection OS-Applications enabled the Error Hook.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: IOC ReceiverIDs not respected in Optimized IOC mode.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: IOC ReceiverIDs operate correctly in AUTOSAR IOC mode, but not when the IOC Optimization mode is enabled. The code has been updated so that ReceiverIDs work in both modes.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Error in BSWMD for Os_TimingFaultDetected element
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: BSWMD EXECUTION-CONTEXT for Os_TimingFaultDetected changed from CAT1 to INTERRUPT-CAT-1

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible lockup in GUI build page
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The GUI could lock up if an odd number of quote characters were entered manually in the build command line.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible GUI fault loading ARXML
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The GUI would show a fault dialog changing the target device if OsRTATargetName was missing from the originally loaded ARXML configuration.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect reference paths in VSMD files
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: ETAS extensions to AUTOSAR ECUC file content are described in the VSMD files that ship with RTA-OS. The files for some versions of the VSMD files had incorrect references, and these are now fixed.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Avoid emitting the same warning or error message more than once.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adds support for AUTOSAR 4.6.0 (R20-11).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Up to 63 events are supported per TASK. Previously 32.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added note that only documented OS APIs are considered to be public. Functions and data in OS header files that are not documented should not be used in non-OS code.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: GetTaskActivationTime now returns E_OS_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS if Value references an invalid address (EXTENDED mode).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Checking for cross-core notifications is faster and is moved to core-local code where appropriate.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added OS option '-{}-incremental'. File saves will not overwrite an existing file if its contents are identical.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Improved Configuration Summary content. Includes RESOURCE core assignments and OS object indexes.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Improved IOC Summary content. Includes supplied IOC configuration options.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/10
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The implementation of the OS_TASKTYPE_TO_INDEX and OS_ISRTYPE_TO_INDEX macros have been changed to avoid MISRA warnings in customer code.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/11
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Allow selection of options 'core-local data' and 'always clear OS data' at the same time. Previously this could cause compilation to fail.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/12
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Do not use VAR() in declaration of structure members. e.g. terminate_jump_buf in Os_TaskDynType.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/13
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Do not use FUNC() in typedefs. e.g. Os_CounterStartType.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/14
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The default implementation of the TASK and ISR macros can be changed by predefining them before Os.h. (May be useful in some tests.)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/15
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: An extra interrupt lock is added to SuspendOSInterrupts() to protect against a possible race condition if it is called by Category 1 ISRs. (Category 1 ISRs never need to call SuspendOSInterrupts() because they already run at a high interrupt priority.)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/16
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The file Os_ScheduleQ.c is omitted from the OS library unless needed because the 'Optimize Schedule()' option is TRUE.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/17
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS variable Os_lock_apperrorhook is only declared when it is used in the library.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/18
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Documentation is updated to state that E_OS_MISSINGEND can be emitted in STANDARD mode for TerminateTask().

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/19
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Documentation is updated to state that GetAlarm only returns E_OS_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS in EXTENDED mode.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/20
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Documentation is updated to add a caution in StartScheduleTableRel() usage.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/21
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Documentation is updated to add a note in CallTrustedFunction() related to timing protection.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/22
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Changed messages and reports from the OS library to use the spelling OS-Application (with the hyphen) more consistently.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/23
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: ORTI files contain correct TASK references in single-core targets.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/24
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Corrected the schemaLocation in BSWMD reports for AUTOSAR versions 4.3.1 onwards.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/25
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added memory sections in BSWMD report that were previously omitted because they were local to individual C files.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/26
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Some IOC messages have changed from warnings to information.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/27
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS will not emit warnings for trusted functions created by RTA-OS to support IOC code.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/28
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Initialization of the IOC is faster for cases where there are many cross-core notifications.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/29
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: OS options 'Omit Default Implementations' and 'Omit Default IOC copy' can be used to omit the default implementation of Os_ioc_memcpy from the library.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/30
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: OS options 'Omit Default Implementations' and 'Omit Default IOC clear' can be used to omit the default implementation of Os_ioc_memcclr from the library.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/31
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: OS option 'IOC Atomic Copy Size' has been added to support (rare) cases where the compiler can emit code that will atomically copy values larger than integer size.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/32
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adds support for IOC ReceiverId that was included in AUTOSAR R20-11.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.2.0/33
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The IOC API function IocInit() is added. It does not have to be called by user code. The OS will correctly initialize the IOC.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation fails on targets that support Enhanced Isolation if the Single Memory Protection Zone is selected.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Selecting Single Memory Protection Zone would remove one of the C types needed to support the Enhanced Isolation code.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.3/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: As 64 bit versions of the tools are used by default, equivalent 32 bit versions are provided for compatibility with older PCs. These are named rtaosgen32.exe and rtaoscfg32.exe.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.3/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The ORTI Api ID is correctly now reset on exit from TerminateTask(). Previously it only got reset in error cases.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.3/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: An unnecessary IOC warning has been removed. The warning saying that a sender has the same OS-Application as a receiver is no longer emitted if there are multiple senders because that is a valid use-case.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: RTA-OS GUI choice of XSD when creating a new project.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When using the GUI to create a new project, the schemaLocation in the first line did not use the updated AUTOSAR schema ending 00044, 00046 and 00048 for AUTOSAR versions 4.3.1, 4.4.0 and 4.5.0 respectively. This has been corrected.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: GUI support for editing OsIsrPeriod and OsTaskPeriod
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: These were added in AUTOSAR 4.5.0 for use by the RTE. The OS does not use them. The RTA-OS GUI now provides support to configure them.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: ApplicationType and value of INVALID_OSAPPLICATION
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: AUTOSAR version 4.5.0 (19-11) requires ApplicationType to start at 0 and INVALID_OSAPPLICATION to be above the range of valid ApplicationTypes. RTA-OS has previously used 0 for INVALID_OSAPPLICATION. To avoid breaking existing code, the new OS option 'ApplicationType starts at zero' should be used if you want this behavior.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: MemoryStartAddressType definition change.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: AUTOSAR version 4.5.0 (19-11) requires MemoryStartAddressType to be of type void *. RTA-OS has previously used uint8 * because that makes it easy to add a MemorySizeType to it. Adding MemorySizeType to a void * is not possible without a cast in portable C. To avoid breaking existing code, the new OS option 'MemoryStartAddressType as void pointer' should be used if you want MemoryStartAddressType to be of type void *.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The engine used in the OS tools to generate the OS code has been updated. Code generation will be faster on current versions of Windows.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.2/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Internal support has been added to support parallelization of compilation (where possible). Future target ports will need to be updated to take advantage of this.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.2/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: 64 bit versions of the tools are used by default. These can access more memory so can cope with bigger input configurations.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.2/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The tools no longer need .NET 3.5 to be installed on the host PC, provided that version 4.7 or above of the .NET Framework is present. (Note that Windows 10 version 1511 and lower cannot install 4.7 so will need .NET 35 support.)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.2/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Minor adjustment in character escaping in the XML and HTML versions of the Options Report.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.2/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: VSMD files have been updated and excess information has been removed.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: BSWMD report did not include IOC-generated memory sections.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Sections created as part of IOC generation are now included.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible fault in generation of OS_INDEX_FOR_{CAT2-ISR-NAME}
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In the case where a Category 1 and Category 2 ISR share the same priority, the value of OS_INDEX_FOR_{CAT2-ISR-NAME} would be wrong if the name of the Category 1 ISR was alphabetically earlier.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Optimized IOC generation crash with unexpected input.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The code generation could fail with an IOC configuration containing an OsIocWriteGroup without any OsIocWriteGroupParams (no data to transfer). The generation now succeeds and a warning is emitted.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.1/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The example code in the documentation for AdvanceCounter was corrected (State.Delay => State->Delay).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.1/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The documentation for the hardware counter callback Os_Cbk_State_() was updated to reflect how it is currently used.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.1/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Parts of the the RTA-OS tooling have been updated to use more recent releases of the .NET Framework. The tools now use .Net version 4.7.2. Experimental 64-bit versions of rtaosgen and rtaoscfg are provided, but RTA-OS targets need to be updated before they will be recognized by these tools.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible compilation error when a hardware counter is attached only to alarms that set events.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In a multi-core system, some compilers could report an error in systems with hardware counters that only had event-setting alarms attached.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Issues in BSWMD file
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In some configurations variable and constants could get emitted as if they were in CODE memory sections. There was also a capitalization error in ResConsumption.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: IOC declaration for ReceiverPullCB might be in wrong memory section.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In IOC configurations with receive callbacks and OS option 'ioc_code' set to 'Caller' the extern for the trusted function did not move to the caller's memory section.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: RTA-OS GUI fails to display Application CoreRef in cases where the CoreRef was previously undefined.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When the GUI created a new CoreRef entry for an OS Application, it would not correctly update the UI to show it. The reference was stored in the ARXML, so saving the file and reloading would correct the issue. Also there was a slight error in the reference definition for the added CoreRef.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation error in CallTrustedFunction.c with -{}-ioc:stub option.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: There could be a compilation error in CallTrustedFunction.c for some targets when using the -{}-ioc:stub command-line option if the configuration did not include some trusted functions. Note that this fix has not been tested on all previously released target versions, so for completeness you should ensure that your testing adequately checks the correct operation of trusted functions if you use the -{}-ioc:stub configuration.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Support has been added for AUTOSAR 4.5.0 (AUTOSAR R19-11).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: It has been noted that TASKs in real-world full-stack AUTOSAR systems are often not written in such a way that they can safely cope with being terminated part way through execution. If the OS sees that the configuration relates to a full-stack AUTOSAR system it will remove the ability to terminate part-completed tasks unless you set the OS option 'Allow forced TASK terminations' to TRUE to confirm that this is safe to do. This means that errors such as timing budget overruns will result in shutdown or controlled reset of the ECU rather than task terminations. This replaces 'Allow RTE TASK terminations'.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS option 'Always clear OS data' has been added so that any OS data normally placed in NO_INIT memory sections is moved to CLEARED memory sections.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added E_OS_SYS_ERROR_LIMIT to mark limit of expected StatusType values.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Os.h can be #included directly in C++ files for compilation. Note that not all target ports are currently compatible with C++ compilation.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The TYPE-EMITTER value in an IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE is used to determine which header files to include in Ioc.h.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: OS Option 'Task Termination Hook' can be used to cause Os_Cbk_TaskTerminated(TaskID) to get called at Task termination.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The command-line option '-{}-ar_version:x.y.z' has been added to rtaosgen.exe to specify the active AUTOSAR version. This overrides the version seen in the ARXML configuration.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A sample file has been added to help show how to specify core IDs in AUTOSAR 4.2 and above. Use option -{}-samples:[EcucCoreDefinition].

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/10
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The name and version of the target port get output in the rtaosgen signon message.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/11
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Removed an unnecessary precautionary check in ActivateTask for multicore systems without crosscore activations.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/12
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: CheckObjectAccess did not detect being passed INVALID_OSAPPLICATION.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/13
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: StartCore did not need to check for non-OS cores when all cores are OS cores.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/14
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: StartNonAutosarCore code eliminated when all cores are OS cores.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/15
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Other small optimizations in cases where there are no Non-Autosar cores.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/16
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Some E_OS_ACCESS checks eliminated in systems where Applications cannot be terminated.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/17
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: GetVersionInfo could only be accessed as Os_GetVersionInfo.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/18
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Speed/memory optimization for the generation of ScheduleTables with expiries that set many Events.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/19
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MISRA report refactored for speed. Especially relevant for systems with many MemMap sections.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/20
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Eliminated a compiler warning in IOC stub mode for fully trusted systems.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/21
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Eliminated a compiler warning relating to pointers to const psets that was seen on some compilers when there were more than 32 tasks on a core.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/22
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: RTA-OS GUI IOC Summary Report can now be run without rebuilding the ECU.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/23
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Error E_OS_CORE can be passed to ErrorHook if an Alarm or ScheduleTable tries to activate a Task on a core that is in shutdown.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/24
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Error E_OS_ACCESS can be passed to ErrorHook if an Alarm or ScheduleTable tries to activate a Task on belonging to an OsApplication that is not accessible.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/25
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The Address field of ApplicationContext argument in the Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess callback now always contains the starting stack value. Previously it was zero unless StackMonitoring was enabled. (Note that the Size field will still be zero unless StackMonitoring is enabled.)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/26
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The adjustment of the MemMap section names based on the AUTOSAR version is modified slightly to be more AUTOSAR compliant.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/27
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The Os_BSWMD.arxml report has been enhanced based on customer feedback.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/28
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Parts of the the RTA-OS tooling have been updated to use more recent releases of the .NET Framework. The tools now use .Net version 4.5.2.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/29
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The version of the ETAS License Manager that is provided has been updated to v1.8.0.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.1.0/30
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The definition of RUNNINGTASK in the ORTI file has been corrected in single-core configurations.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible compilation warning in 6.0.0
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In configurations that prevent forced task terminations there might be a compiler warning in ProtectionSupport.c because the the local variable 'Application' was initialized but not used. Depending on compiler options this might be interpreted as a compilation failure.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible compilation error when a software counter is attached only to alarms that set events.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In a multi-core system, some compilers could report an error in systems with software counters that only had event-setting alarms attached.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Race condition when using SuspendOSInterrupts in a CAT1 ISR
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If a CAT1 ISR preempted the SuspendOSInterrupts API at a particular point, and then itself called SuspendOSInterrupts, it was possible for it to disrupt the final call to ResumeOSInterrupts such that the interrupt priority would not be restored. Although there is no reason for a Category 1 interrupt to call SuspendOSInterrupts, AUTOSAR allows it. The code is now modified when there are CAT1 ISRs in the configuration so that this issue cannot occur.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.0.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Small refinements and corrections to the preview IOC code in 5.91.0.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.0.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Split internal Ioc.c file into two files to reduce memory requirements during code generation.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 6.0.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: RTA-RTE does not support termination of its tasks part way through execution. If the OS sees that the configuration contains RTE-related information it will now remove the ability to terminate part-completed tasks unless the OS option 'Allow RTE TASK terminations' is set to TRUE. This means that errors such as timing budget overruns will result in shutdown or controlled reset of the ECU.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible deadlock in IOC
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: There was a small race condition where preemption of an IOC write by higher priority IOC-accessing code might result in a spinlock-related deadlock. The issue only applied when preempting an IOC write where there was a cross-core callback function that was triggered by the write and the data type was primitive and unqueued.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.1/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MemMap sections containing the text 'CODE_LIB' are changed to contain the text 'CODE' for AUTOSAR versions 4.1 and above.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.1/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The MemMap '{CN}_CODE' callout style introduced for AUTOSAR version 4.1 is kept to 4.1 only. It is removed from 4.2 and above. The MappingCodeLocation feature can be used for callouts above version 4.1.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.1/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MemMap sections containing the text 'COREn_CODE' or 'COREn_CODE_LIB' are changed to contain the 'COREn' part at the end.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.1/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MemMap sections containing the text 'COREn' have the additional modifier '_LOCAL' added at the end for AUTOSAR 4.3 versions and above.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When OS option 'IOC Data' is set to 'Writer' the IOC code can optimize communications containing data types are copyable atomically - e.g. uint32. Fewer interrupt locks will be taken and some calls may become inlinable macros.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The implementation for IOC queued communications has been changed to remove the need for interrupt and cross-core locks to manage the queue data. When OS option 'IOC Data' is set to 'Writer' some queue control data is assigned to the writer's OS Application and some to the reader's.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: To support the removal of locking in IOC queues, IOCEmptyQueue_xxx APIs are now specified such that they should not be called if corresponding read/writes could occur at the same time.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The AUTOSAR TotalNumberOfCores value has been reimplemented as Os_TotalNumberOfCores so that all OS data starts with an Os_ prefix. A macro TotalNumberOfCores maps on to Os_TotalNumberOfCores so no code changes are needed.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Some explanations in MISRA reports have been updated for clarity.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.91.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: VSMD files can be created for specific versions of OS and target plugins. This is primarily to support ETAS ISOLAR tooling.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect return from Os_GetCurrentTPL()
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The return value from Os_GetCurrentTPL() did not take account of the raising of the task priority when a resource lock was in effect. It could also produce incorrect results in systems with shared task priorities.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible RES_SCHEDULER failure in multicore systems
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In systems with a mixture of AUTOSAR and non-AUTOSAR cores, locking RES_SCHEDULER (when enabled) would fail on any AUTOSAR core that had an ID greater than any non-AUTOSAR core.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.7.3/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: No changes since 5.7.2 preview.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation error with -{}-ioc:impl
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In multicore systems with cross-core IOC Rx callbacks, the code in Ioc.c could fail to compile or, depending on the compiler, call an illegal function.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect re-initialization of some OS data during a warm reset.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS-internal Os_AnyCoreInfo data structure that is used in multi-core systems was in a 'NO INIT' memory section but should have been in a 'CLEARED' memory section. It would normally be cleared correctly on a first-time reset by the C startup code but in systems with a warm reset capability it was not getting reinitialized to zero.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect scheduling with ECC2 tasks and cross-core SetEvent.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If SetEvent was called for an ECC2 task on a different core at the point that it was entering the Waiting state, the dispatcher could try to start an erroneous task.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: No autostarted Alarms or ScheduleTables when ServiceProtection configured.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The checks of caller legality were getting wrongly applied in the OS startup code, causing autostarted Alarms and ScheduleTables to get rejected.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.7.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The macro OS_INDEX_FOR_{taskname} has been added. This can be useful in switch/case statements.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.7.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The macro OS_INDEX_FOR_{isrname} has been added. This can be useful in switch/case statements.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.7.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Allows time intervals to be used in the Schedule Table Assistant GUI.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.7.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The definition of RUNNINGTASK in the ORTI file has been modified slightly in multi-core configurations to make it easier for debuggers to support TOTRACE operation.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible compilation error in a Custom DLL
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In a customer-specific DLL it was possible to see a compilation error in some situations unless ORTI generation was enabled.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.90/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: SetRelAlarm and SetAbsAlarm will return E_OS_CALLEVEL if Service Protection is enabled and they are called from Startup Hook. This is to conform to the AUTOSAR specification.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.90/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Much larger IOC MCSD files can be processed.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.90/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Support has been added for AUTOSAR 4.4.0, including the APIs ActivateTaskAsyn and SetEventAsyn. Note that OS option 'Support ActivateTaskAsyn and SetEventAsyn' has to be used to enable them.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.90/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Where there is ECUC partition information present in the ARXML configuration, the RTA-OS GUI allows OS Applications to select the partition that they relate to.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.90/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MEMORY-SECTION information has been added to the Os_BSWMD.arxml report.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.90/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added new OS option 'Omit Default Implementations'. RTA-OS normally provides default implementations for Os_Cbk_Idle, Os_Cbk_InShutdown and Os_Cbk_StackOverrunHook in the generated library. This option can be used to omit these functions from the library.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: XML identifiers containing underscores
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For AUTOSAR versions 4.0.x to 4.2.1, identifiers in ARXML were not allowed to have 2 consecutive underscores. However from 4.2.2 they are allowed. RTA-OS previously did not allow 2 consecutive underscores in version 4.2.2 and above.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect XML references created when adding objects in the RTA-OS GUI.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The tooling could use the path to a non-OS ECUC-MODULE-DEF element if one appeared in a later ARXML file.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect handling of some references in optimized IOC ReadGroup and WriteGroup containers.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The decoding of references to ReceiverProperties and SenderProperties previously assumed unique SHORTNAMEs. The decoding now correctly uses the full path name, so duplicate SHORTNAMEs can be used.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Task continuing to run after TerminateApplication
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: TerminateApplication might fail to fully terminate lower priority tasks if a cross-core activated tasks was already running on the terminating core.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Custom DLL options show in the RTA-OS GUI even when the Custom DLL is NOT activated.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fixed. These options now only appear when the Custom DLL is activated.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation failure in IocStubs.c.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The code in IocStubs.c would not compile in the case where option -{}-ioc:impl was selected and there was an Rx Callback for a communication that was single core.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: IOC MCSD file processing was slow.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: On large input files, the MSCD file processing was inefficient. This has been corrected.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect MPU handling with ECC tasks when using Trusted-With-Protection.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The MPU was not being switched out on entry to the code that dispatched ECC tasks. This has been corrected.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Sequential TerminateApplication calls might fail
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Where application code called TerminateApplication for several different OS Applications in sequence, calls after the first would fail with E_OS_CALLEVEL if Service Protection was enabled.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.3/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Information about interrupt vectors and target variants has been added to the Options Reference report

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.3/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In the OS GUI rtaoscfg, the interrupt addresses input field in OS Configuration / ISRs / General can have part of the name entered and it will suggest entries that contain this text. This is intended to help select interrupts more easily on larger processors.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.3/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Removed duplicated line 'Os_AppOverride = INVALID_OSAPPLICATION;' that could occur in StartOS.c and ShutdownOS.c depending on configuration. No functional change.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.3/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Removed duplicated line 'Os_AppAccess = 1U;' that could occur in StartOS.c depending on configuration. No functional change.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible failure in cross-core SetRelAlarm
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Where a software counter has a maximum value less than 0xffffffff, calling SetRelAlarm from a core different from the one running the counter could result in the alarm match time being set to a value that would never be reached. Workaround: Use full modulus counter or use SetAbsAlarm.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible code loop with hardware counters
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS-internal function Os_HwQFlush might execute an infinite loop in systems with more than one hardware counter.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: GetTaskID not always callable from Os_Cbk_CheckStackDepth
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: With ServiceProtection turned on, calling GetTaskID() from within the Os_Cbk_CheckStackDepth callback would be refused if the callback was raised for an autostarted task.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Tools reject ARXML files that do not include OS container
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Tools warn about input ARXML files that do not include OS container, but allow them now. This is because in some cases they might contain references to data that is needed to complete the configuration; typically core assignments.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible compilation failure with Enhanced Isolation
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: On some targets there could be a compile error in TerminateApplication.c because the variable 'calling_application' was declared but not used in some configurations.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Possible compilation failure in cross-core interrupts with metrics enabled
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: On some targets there could be a compile error in cross-core interrupt code. A variable was declared but not used in some configurations.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Update ETAS address in About dialogs and documentation.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Supports C28xTI target port (16-bit chars).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Reports missing data references in IOC Data Properties as an error.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Schema and VSMD (ParamDef) added for AUTOSAR 4.3.1.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Support reading non UTF8 MCSD files (IOC)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MemoryMappingCodeLocationRef is supported on Hooks, OsApplication Hooks, Tasks and ISRs. This means that these objects can be located in individually configured MemMap sections.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: AllowAccess can raise E_OS_CALLEVEL

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: ControlIdle can raise E_OS_CALLEVEL

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: GetApplicationState can raise E_OS_CALLEVEL

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: VSMD (ParamDef) files updated for conformance with customer tooling. e.g. alphabetical ordering

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/10
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adjusted internal references in the supplied VSMD (ParamDef) files for each AUTOSAR 4 version.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/11
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: ActivateTask, StartScheduleTableRel, StartScheduleTableAbs, StartScheduleTableSynchron, SyncScheduleTable, Os_SyncScheduleTableRel and Os_SyncScheduleTableRel are not allowed in StartupHook (only checked when Service Protection is configured)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/12
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Rename E_OS_XCORE_QFULL to E_OS_SYS_XCORE_QFULL

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.1/13
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Minor correction in IOC code to ensure that correct MemMap sections are used in declaration and implementation.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect values returned from GetSpinlockInfo.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: GetSpinlockInfo could return an incorrect (large) value for the MaxSpinTime value. The value would be effectively the current stopwatch time. CurrentLockTime could contain a non-zero value when unlocked.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Tested on Windows 10.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Revised BSWMD file based on feedback.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Code generated for StartScheduleTableSynchron didn't always check that the ScheduleTable was explicitly synchronized.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adjusted code and clarified documentation for GetElapsedTime and GetExecutionTime for consistent behavior in Pre and Post TASK hooks.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Updated version of ETAS Licensing Manager is supplied. License controls are now placed in the Auth.exe.config file.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MISRA 2012 11.4/11.6 dismissals added to OSError_ macros.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added check to Schedule() in case it is called from outside a TASK (not an allowed situation).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Corrected the OSError_ macro definition for GetStackSize.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Os_Cbk_CheckMemoryAccess was being passed the size of a pointer, not the size of the pointed-to item when used in some API calls.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/10
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: StartScheduleTableSynchron now correctly returns E_OS_ID when a non-explicitly synchronized table is passed in to it.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/11
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: ReleaseSpinlock now correctly returns E_OS_STATE when passed a NESTABLE spinlock that was not locked by the calling TASK or ISR.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/12
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Peripheral Areas are supported. This is an AUTOSAR 4.3.0 feature. RTA-OS allows the feature to be used with any AUTOSAR version.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/13
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Interrupt Source APIs are supported. This is an AUTOSAR 4.3.0 feature. RTA-OS allows the feature to be used with any AUTOSAR version. Your RTA-OS target port may need to be updated to support this feature.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/14
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A new report 'Os_BSWMD.arxml' has been added to allow the generation of a BSW Module Definition file for RTA-OS.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.6.0/15
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: RTA-OS Custom DLLs must now be enabled via an ETAS-provided key in the Auth.exe.config file.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Added brackets around some macro parameters in Os.h
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Some macros with parameters did not wrap them in brackets when used, contrary to MISRA 2012 rule 20.7. This was not detected by our source code checker.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Could not release spinlocks with interrupts disabled when Service Protection was enabled.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: GetSpinlock, ReleaseSpinlock, TryToGetSpinlock and TerminateApplication can now be called with interrupts disabled.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect MPU settings after preemption by a trusted-with-protection TASK
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: On return from preemption by a trusted-with-protection TASK to an untrusted TASK, the Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess callback was not getting called to reset the MPU to the appropriate values for the untrusted TASK.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Improve command-line error messages
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Extra clarification has been added to some command-line errors to be more specific about the cause of the error. Previously messages such as 'Illegal characters in path' were emitted without indicating which option was affected.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect calculation of RegisterSet preemption depth in multicore systems
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Applied only on multicore where OS option 'Disallow Schedule()' was set and Tasks had same priorities or used internal resources. The size needed for the RegisterSet buffers could be underestimated. Workaround: remove 'Disallow Schedule()'.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.11/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The Reference Guide now shows that E_OS_ILLEGAL_ADDRESS can be reported in both STANDARD and EXTENDED builds.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.11/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adjust IOC code used to copy data between structures so that bit-compatible but differently typed structures can be used.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.11/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: IOC initialization uses structure copies rather than memcpy if possible.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.11/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: ChainTask code adjusted to avoid a possible cross-core race condition that could delay the running of the chained TASK.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: User Guide.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Clarified the description of Stack overheads for ISR and Stack overheads for ISR activation.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.10/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Updated the documentation for Os_Cbk_CheckMemoryAccess to clarify its usage.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.10/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The default implementation of Os_ioc_memcpy in the RTA-OS library can be replaced simply by the application code providing its own version.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.10/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: IOC code will use structure assignment rather than Os_ioc_memcpy when possible.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.10/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The names of the memory sections created for the OS options 'IOC Data' and 'IOC code' has been modified since the preview.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.9/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Each cross-core IOC communication has its own spinlock, rather than a single global one. This is done to reduce cross-core blocking.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.9/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The new OS option 'IOC Data' can be used to control the MemMap data section allocation for IOC data buffers. This can be used to reduce untrusted/trusted code transitions.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.9/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The new OS option 'IOC Code' can be used to control the MemMap code section allocation for IOC APIs. APIs can be allocated to a code section belonging to the OS Application of the caller.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.9/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: IOC code is optimized when the OS detects extra call-pattern information from RTA-RTE. Multiple data transfers can be batched together and executed via a single API call. Preemption information can be used to reduce blocking. This behavior can be disabled using the new OS option 'Disable IOC optimizations'

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.9/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The new OS option 'IOC blocking threshold' can be used to control the blocking duration for batched IOC data transfers.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: ECC TASKs running on the wrong core.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: It was possible for an ECC TASK allocated to one core to be (always) started on a different core. This situation would be quickly noticed in testing. This issue occurred only when more than one core had ECC TASKs, and depended on TASK priorities. Workaround: Ensure that all ECC TASKs on core {N} have priorities greater than those on core {N-1}.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation error in Os_Cfg_Counters.c
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In a multicore system with a Software Counter that only drives Alarms, and where none of the Alarms activate a TASK, a compile error could occur because of a missing reference to os_current_controlled_core.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Insufficient resource locking for TASKs that share a priority.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If a resource is used by a TASK that shares a priority with another TASK, then it should lock to at least the base priority of all TASKs that share that priority. Previously it was possible for the lock to omit some of these shared-priority TASKs. This is only a problem if the TASKs use multiple activation, because the implementation of the resulting FIFO activation mechanism meant that the activation of one of the TASKs with higher base priority could allow a lower base priority TASK to be popped from the FIFO and run, even though the resource was locked. A workaround is to configure the system so that any resource that is used by a TASK with a shared priority is also used by all other TASKs at that priority.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Incorrect return from GetCurrentApplicationID()
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When calling GetCurrentApplicationID() from an ISR or TASK that was preempting a Trusted Function, the return value would be the ID of the OS Application for the Trusted Function.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Imbalance in MemMap start/stop directives in systems with no Counters, Alarms or ScheduleTables.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In a system where no code needed to be emitted in for Counters, Alarms or ScheduleTables, the internal source file Os_Cfg_Counters.c could contain a OS_STOP_SEC_CONST_FAST_UNSPECIFIED without a preceding OS_START_SEC_CONST_FAST_UNSPECIFIED. There is no effect on the generated object code.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Number of Cores incorrect in the RTA-OS GUI
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When a configuration with OsNumberOfCores equal to 1 was first loaded in to the RTA-OS GUI, it would increase the number to match the total number of cores supported by the target. Subsequent loads of the same file did not have this behavior.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.7/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Minor fixed for changes noted in the 5.5.6 preview.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.7/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Additional hardware information has been added to the Configuration Summary.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.7/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Additional information about Alarm autostarting has been added to the Configuration Summary.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.7/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Where a configuration has untrusted code and stack protection is used, targets that use the stack realignment feature now follow the same code path whether the AlignedAddress has been changed or not. This makes the run-time behavior consistent in each invocation and makes it easier to protect from register corruption in the untrusted code.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Failure to build OS (out of memory)
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Migration of core tools to use .NET version 4 has improved memory performance.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Reference bases in IOC
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The IOC component in RTA-OS now takes account of Reference Bases in the ARXML.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Calculation of remaining time is incorrect when a trusted function is terminated with an exception.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The remaining time is now decremented correctly.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Calculation of remaining time is incorrect when a trusted function is terminated with resource lock timeout AND there is a timing protection interrupt.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The remaining time is now decremented correctly.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.6/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: This preview release supports TrustedApplicationWithProtection for OS Applications. This can only be selected on target ports that have been updated to support it.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Os_ShutdownAllCores() fails in untrusted mode when ORTI is switched on
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Os_ShutdownAllCores may only be called by trusted code, so it returns without shutting down when called from untrusted code. Previously the ORTI variable Os_OrtiApiID was written before checking the trust state, possibly provoking a memory protection fault.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.5/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: MISRA checks are now performed according to the MISRA C:2012 Guidelines using the PC-Lint analysis tool. This has resulted in small modifications to individual lines of code, and slightly different lint dismissals. The MISRA Deviations report has been enhanced to include additional information required by MISRA C:2012.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.5/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Autostarted TASKs, Alarms and ScheduleTables are now started on the core that owns them. Previously the master core started all of them.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.5/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS options 'Asynchronous TASK activation' and 'AsyncQ' have been added. These modify APIs that cause cross-core activation (e.g. ActivateTask, ChainTask and SetEvent) so that they queue a message for the owning core to process during a cross-core interrupt. This allows the OS to remove the internal task-related spinlock and reduce cross-core blocking. Refer to the RTA-OS Reference Guide for more information.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.5/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The new OS option '-{}-ioc' has been added. This now allows the OS and IOC to be generated in independent stages. Refer to the RTA-OS Reference Guide for more information.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Cross-core IocWrite must be implemented as a FUNCTION if there is a receive callback
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: RTA-OS will try to implement IocWrite as a MACRO for simple types so that the code can be inlined for efficiency. However prior to this fix, the implementation would not signal a receiver callback to run.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Build-related command-line options to GUI
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The -{}-target_include option could be passed to the GUI, but it did not get passed automatically to rtaosgen for building, although it could be entered manually and stored in the .rtaos file. The code now passes through GUI options that relate to the build. Affected versions: 5.5.0 and 5.5.1.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: E_OS_VALUE is documented as an error return code for GetElapsedCounterValue. (Documentation change only.)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.2/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A counter with no Alarms or ScheduleTables will wrap at its maxallowedvalue. Previously it would continue incrementing to the maximum 32-bit value before wrapping.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Issue with IocEmptyQueue APIs on PPC target ports
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When a queued communication is used with IOC between exactly 2 different cores, the code generated for the IocEmptyQueue_xxx API should only be called from the receiver. If it is called by the sender then it might change the interrupt priority of the core of the receiver. This only affects multicore PowerPC target ports.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Fault in multicore code for Delayed Tasks
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The code generated for Delayed Tasks in a multicore configuration might only deal with one core. Affected versions: 5.5.0 and 5.5.1.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation error in Os_Cfg.c with shared resources
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A compilation error would happen in Os_Cfg.c if the following 2 conditions occurred: (a) a resource was used by an interrupt and (b) the number of tasks on two cores differed by 32 or more. Affected versions: 5.5.0 and 5.5.1.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.1/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adds the AUTOSAR 4.2.x 'TrustedApplicationDelayTimingViolationCall' feature. RTA-OS allows this option to be selected for both trusted and untrusted OS Applications. RTA-OS also allows it to be used with earlier AUTOSAR versions.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Race condition affecting per-application error hooks
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For multicore configurations using the RH850GHS, PPCWR, PPCHT, PPCGHS and ZynqRVDS targets, it was possible for one core to raise the wrong application-specific error hook if (1) two such hooks were raised at the same time on different cores and (2) both belonged to an untrusted OS-Application and (3) untrusted stack realignment was configured.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Supports AUTOSAR 4.2.1 and 4.2.2, apart from 'TrustedApplicationDelayTimingViolationCall' and 'TrustedApplicationWithProtection' which are not yet implemented.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A new Options Reference Report is available, and the OS and target options are described in the RTA-OS Reference Guide.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Addition of the OS_TPL_/OS_IMASK_ macros and the Os_GetCurrentTPL()/Os_GetCurrentIMask() APIs.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The internal implementation for multicore projects has been changed to reduce cross-core interference and to improve scalability to a higher number of cores. The ready state of tasks is now owned by the appropriate core rather then being shared between all cores. Each core has an internal spinlock for protecting access to its tasks, which reduces cross-core blocking. The task dispatching code is now specific to each core, so it only depends on the number of tasks on the core rather than the whole system.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS no longer uses *_least types in public APIs.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The DelayedTask feature is core-specific. You must only add tasks from the calling core to a delayed taskset.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: DelayedTasks are only supported in systems with a maximum of 64 tasks per core.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS option Timing Protection Interrupt is now a TRUE/FALSE value. Previously a Category 1 ISR had to be specified. Each core must still provide a timing protection interrupt when this option is set to TRUE, but the OS does not check it.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: OS builds that are done inside the RTA-OS GUI are now implemented by building a command-line and passing it to rtaosgen.exe for the actual build. This avoids out of memory issues that have been seen in GUI builds. Note that the command-line can be modified in the GUI before starting the build.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.5.0/10
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The ShutdownAllCores API uses an inlined version of DisableAllInterrupts, instead of calling it directly.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Ordering in GUI
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In the navigation tree, ISRs can be sorted by Name, Priority, Address or Category. Tasks can be sorted by Name or Priority.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Sample Applications
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Sample applications contain the version and processor variant in the sample XML, so that they can be more easily read back into the GUI.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: GUI import
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: There was a discrepancy in the ISR information retained when opening a configuration file via the GUI versus the command line.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Sample code
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The default implementations of Os_Cbk_SetTimeLimit and Os_Cbk_SuspendTimeLimit are multicore aware.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: GUI build option
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The RTA-OS GUI option "Keep source code" was greyed out even when there was a valid licence.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Tools crash
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The tools could crash when loading a configuration where an OS Application did not have a valid core assignment.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Documentation
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The User Documentation adds Windows 8.1 to the list of supported platforms

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: RTA-OS User Guide
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Additional explanation of which APIs can be called when interrupts are disabled.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: RTA-OS User Guide
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The description of ECC Wait Stack size is clarified.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Hardware Counters
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: It was possible to miss the activation of an alarm if a new alarm was being started and the hardware compare match occurred just after the OS had checked if the compare interrupt was pending.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Timing protection restoration after nested preemption
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: It was possible for a nested interrupt to allocate more execution time to a preempted TASK than was correct. Only the first preemption should adjust its time.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.4/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: OS option 'Collect OS usage metrics' has been added. Please refer to RTA-OS User Guide

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.4/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Support for TriCoreHighTec optimizations

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.3/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Multicore only: Resource lock durations specified for RES_SCHEDULER are recognized. Previously they were ignored.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.3/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Hardware counters can be used reliably on multicore systems. The OS now ensures that the callbacks that change the counter match conditions only run on the core that owns the counter. This makes it easier to implement correct callbacks. Typically the same code can be used for both single and multi core systems.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.3/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: ORTI output is modified to change from using the pointer variables Os_OrtiRunningISRn, Os_OrtiRunningTaskn to a more explicit form. Some debuggers didn't like having to dereference the variables.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: OS variables read permission with untrusted code
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Just after switching to untrusted mode to enter an untrusted TASK, ISR or function, the OS was reading from an OS variable or constant that held the entry address. This meant that OS data had to be readable (though not writable) from untrusted code. Although this is a common way to set up the memory protection, some customers might prefer to prevent read access to OS data by untrusted code. The implementation has been adjusted to help support this.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: ShutdownAllCores with untrusted OS Applications
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Calling ShutdownAllCores could fail to complete if code on another core was running untrusted code at the time of the call.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Check-only build option in GUI
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For some targets (e.g. TriCore Tasking), a check-only build of the OS from within the OS GUI would fail if the compiler was not on the path.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation warning in CallTrustedFunction.c
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A compilation warning could occur in CallTrustedFunction.c for the case where memory protection is enabled and there are untrusted TASKs, but no untrusted Functions.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation failure in Os_Wrapper.c
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If the OS option 'Only Terminate Untrusted Applications' was true and there were no Untrusted Applications, this could provoke a compilation error. This is corrected and now for this case the OS does not emit any code to support termination of Applications.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation failure in Ioc.c
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A compilation error could occur in Ioc.c for elements taking 2 or more values where the FunctionImplementationKind was MACRO.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Write to ORTI variable from untrusted code
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Some 'readonly' APIs such as GetISRID() were not switching to trusted mode before updating the OS internal ORTI variables. This occurred when ORTI was enabled and the option 'Untrusted code can read OS data' was TRUE.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation failure in TimeProtection.c
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A compilation error could occur in TimeProtection.c when using Timing Protection with a timing interrupt in a multicore configuration.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Race condition in ShutdownAllCores
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If a cross-core interrupt occurred during a call to ShutdownAllCores, it was possible that the other cores would not be informed about the shutdown immediately. In some situations the other cores might not detect the shutdown. This issue is fixed. As a workaround for earlier versions, call DisableAllInterrupts() before calling ShutdownAllCores().

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fix an issue in multicore projects that broke backwards compatibility with older target ports.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.2/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Remove declaration of unused variable setjmp_retval in cross-core ISRs.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.1/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When 'Optimize for core-local memory' is selected, the per-core dynamic data for ISRs is now initialized correctly during StartOS. Previously it relied on its memory being cleared during startup.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.1/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added protective ()'s in the macros OSMEMORY_IS_READABLE, OSMEMORY_IS_WRITEABLE, OSMEMORY_IS_EXECUTABLE and OSMEMORY_IS_STACKSPACE

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.1/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: GUI supports creation of sample applications for VRTA version 3.0.13. The code did not previously support versions that ended with more than one digit.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: GUI crash
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Malformed ARXML missing VALUE-REFs could cause the GUI to crash when reading in the data.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Untrusted functions
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: RTA-OS allows untrusted OS Applications to declare 'Trusted Functions'. These can be used in the same way as AUTOSAR trusted functions, but they run untrusted. A build warning has been added to make it clear when this is happening.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.0/1
