RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The OS option 'Optimize for core-local memory' has been added. This re-organizes some OS data structures so that they can be assigned to core-local memory via the MemMap mechanism. Refer to the sample Os_MemMap.h to see the section names that get allocated.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The initialization of internal spinlock variables has been moved inside StartOS rather than relying on power-on clearing of their values.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For AUTOSAR 4.0.2 and above, OS data that must be cleared during reset moves from MemMap section OS_START_SEC_VAR_POWER_ON_INIT to OS_START_SEC_VAR_CLEARED.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For AUTOSAR 4.0.3 and above, OS data that does not need to be initialized during reset moves from MemMap section OS_START_SEC_VAR_NOINIT to OS_START_SEC_VAR_NO_INIT.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The content of the example Os_MemMap.h file is adjusted in AUTOSAR 4.0.2 and 4.0.3 configurations to match the changes in the MemMap specification.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.0/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For AUTOSAR 4.0 and below, switch from using OS_APPL_CODE to OS_CALLOUT_CODE in non OS-internal functions to be AUTOSAR 4.0 compliant. (OS_CALLOUT_CODE will fall back to be the same as OS_APPL_CODE for earlier versions, so no change is needed in your code.)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.0/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For AUTOSAR 4.1, the memory mapping mechanism #includes the Os_MemMap.h file rather than MemMap.h.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.0/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For AUTOSAR 4.1, callouts (including hooks) have their own memory mapping section named according to the pattern OS_START_SEC_{CN}_CODE where {CN} is the callback name written in uppercase letters. The Os_MemMap.h sample file can be used to see the expected names. Note that some existing target port plug-ins might not compile for AUTOSAR 4.1 because of this change. Please contact support if this is the case for you.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.4.0/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: VSMD files have been updated.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.91/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Supports for AUTOSAR 4.1.3

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.91/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Adjust OSTICKDURATION macro so it can be used in the code '#if (OSTICKDURATION < 1000)'

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.91/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The file Os_MemMap.h has moved into the RTA-OS samples. Use command-line option -{}-samples:[Includes] to generate it. This is to emphasize that it is an example that can be optionally used as a start point by a system integrator.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Missed events from alarm
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION:
Corrected an issue that could result in alarms not setting events under these specific conditions: IF (you have alarms that set events) AND there are alarms on the same counter that activate tasks but *don't* set events AND EITHER ((number of event bits < 9) AND ((number of tasks + number of alarm callbacks + number of event bits) > 8)) OR ((number of event bits < 17) AND ((number of tasks + number of alarm callbacks + number of event bits) > 16)) THEN One or more events will not get set when the alarm fires. The task may resume from wait, but will not see the event set.
Note: 'number of event bits' is normally the maximum number of events that any task waits for. It is not the total number of events. i.e. you can have 30 events in the system, but if no task waits for more than 2 events then the number of event bits is 2. This only changes if you allocate specific mask values to alarms because the number of event bits may have to increase to account for the value you set.
Workaround: Select an event and force its mask to bit 9 or 17 such that the condition above is no longer met.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Software counters do not increment unless they contain Alarms or ScheduleTables
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: (Introduced in version 5.3.1) Software counters that contain no Alarms or ScheduleTables returned zero from GetCounterValue().

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: VSMD reference in OsRegSetRef
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The DESTINATION-REF for OsRegSet has a wrong path

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: VSMD REFINED-MODULE-DEF-REF
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The REFINED-MODULE-DEF-REF section was in the wrong place in the VSMD

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: User Guide
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Section 10.1 of the RTA-OS User Guide wrongly stated that a Counter could only drive one ScheduleTable.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.90/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Experimental mc_experiment option for allocating data to core-local memory

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.90/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Cross-core access to hardware counters from Alarms/ScheduleTables on different cores is not allowed unless option 'Allow cross-core HW counter access' is set. This is because it is generally not possible to eliminate race conditions with code on different cores trying to set a new counter match value at the same time.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: API GetElapsedValue is added for AUTOSAR 4.x applications. It is an alias to GetElapsedCounterValue.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: In version 5.3.1, the VSMD files for AUTOSAR 3.x were generated incorrectly and included some AUTOSAR 4.x syntax.
In version 5.3.1 an internal crash could occur when calculating stack usage. This affected unusual multicore configurations where the OsTaskCanCallSchedule feature was set to FALSE on a TASK.
Modification 5.3.1/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Support for AUTOSAR 4.1.x ARXML.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: IOC supports N:M unqueued communication. This is a feature of AUTOSAR 4.1.x, but RTA-OS supports it in earlier versions when configured.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The boolean parameter OsTaskCanCallSchedule is added to the TASK configuration. This can be set to FALSE if the TASK will not call the Schedule() API call - which may reduce system stack usage. The parameter defaults to TRUE. It is ignored if global OS option 'Disallow Schedule()' is TRUE, because that implies that no TASK calls Schedule().

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Option '-{}-debug:compact_source' can be used to emit the compact version of the source code used to build the OS. This version does not expand all include files into the C source. (Source code license needed).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Option 'Stack Sampling' has been added to cause the Os_Cbk_CheckStackDepth callback to get called to support monitoring of overall stack usage at run time

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When you create a new project in RTAOSCfg, you can now provide an empty ECU Configuration Name. In this case no ECUC-VALUE-COLLECTION element will be placed in the generated ARXML file. Similarly, if a file is read in without an ECUC-VALUE-COLLECTION element, none will be created.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: OS Option 'Task Activation Hook' can be used to cause Os_Cbk_TaskActivated(TaskID) to get called at Task activations.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The memmap section for certain constants has moved from CONST_UNSPECIFIED to CONST_FAST_UNSPECIFIED. The constants affected are Os_const_coreconfiguration, Os_const_applications, Os_const_isrs, Os_const_tasks, Os_const_resources, Os_const_alarms, Os_const_counters, Os_const_scheduletables and TotalNumberOfCores.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Os_Cbk_StackOverrunHook: OS_ECC_START, OS_ECC_RESUME and OS_ECC_WAIT can occur independently of whether Stack Monitoring is configured.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/10
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Updates/corrections to the documentation for Os_Cbk_StackOverrunHook.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/11
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Changes license check scheme.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/12
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Removed E_OS_ACCESS checks from SyncScheduleTable/SyncScheduleTableRel when in STANDARD status.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/13
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For configurations created for AUTOSAR 4.0.3 onwards, references in the ARXML change from /ETAS_RTAOS to /AUTOSAR_Os/EcucModuleDefs

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/14
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When creating a new project in the GUI, the Package Heirarchy can be used to create nested packages. e.g. a/b/c/EcucModuleConfigurationValuess.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/15
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Only for multicore systems using delayed tasks: the task dispatcher is modified to avoid a race condition involving changing the delayed task set from a different core.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.1/16
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Structure of VSMD files for AUTOSAR 4.0.3+ has changed slightly, based on customer feedback.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Compilation error in CallTrustedFunction.c with a rare set of multicore configuration options.
In SC3/4, PostTaskHook was not getting called when a Task terminated through ChainTask.
In the Beta2 GUI, sorting of some columns in the TASK and ISR summary caused the application to crash.
Modification 5.3.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Correction to max execution time calculation for ECC tasks.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.3.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Correct service protection checks for APIs that can run during Startup Hook

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When the GUI RTAOSCfg creates new containers with an upper-multiplicity of 1, it now standardizes on giving then a SHORT-NAME that is the same as the AUTOSAR name. e.g. OsHooks, OsScheduleTblAdjustableExpPoint

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added option to control checking for Activation of ECC tasks that are in the WAITING state. By default the check is not done, because ECC tasks typically are only activated once.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Update to the code used to support Elapsed Time Recording to fix issue with ISRs that activate tasks.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The Os_SyncScheduleTableRel() has been added as an alternative for the AUTOSAR SyncScheduleTable() API where you supply a drift value rather than an absolute value.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Option 'Enable Elapsed Time Recording' has been added to provide support for measuring total time spent in Idle, Tasks and Category 2 ISRs through a new set of APIs

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Option 'Single Memory Protection Zone' has been added to optimize setting up memory protection where all untrusted code can use a single set of protection settings..

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Option 'Stack Only Memory Protection' has been added to optimize setting up memory protection where only the stack protection range needs to be changed.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/8
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Option 'Optimize Schedule()' is now also effective when there are more than 64 tasks.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/9
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If it exists, the file (projectname)_postsamples.bat gets run during the build process. It runs after any sample files get created, so can be used to copy them to the correct build location.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/10
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The StackFaultHook option is taken account of correctly.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/11
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The version number of the target port is added to the header of all source files.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/12
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The command-line option -{}-os_option has been added. This acts like -{}-target_option, but for the OS-specific options.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/13
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Stack realignment of ECC tasks is possible within Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess().

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/14
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: OS_INDEX_TO_TASKTYPE has been added as a partner to OS_TASKTYPE_TO_INDEX

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/15
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The macro OS_FAST_TASK_TERMINATION is defined when option 'Fast Terminate' results in TerminateTask() being implemented as a simple return statement.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/16
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: In ARXML for AUTOSAR 4.0.3, OsResources do not belong to an OS-Application.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.9/17
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Clarifications in documentation about when spinlocks can be used.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: In RTAOSCfg, saving with an empty expiry point selected could cause unhandled exception
In RTAOsCfg, checking is done that the correct number of stack values are entered. Affects TriCore targets.
Modification 5.2.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Prevents a spurious call to Os_Cbk_StackOverrunHook that could occur with Stack monitoring. This could only occur when using TerminateTask to leave an ECC task, and then only if an interrupt occurred within a small execution window.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: RTAOSCfg: Saving the project with the cursor in an empty cell on the ScheduleTable/Expiry Points pane could cause an unhandled exception
RTAOSCfg: The validation of stack values for multi-stack targets (TriCore) did not reject entries with fewer stack values than required. e.g. 16 instead of 16,64.

When starting an ECC task, there was a small window where an ISR could interrupt and cause the stack overrun hook to be called when it was not necessary. Only affected systems with stack monitoring enabled.
Fixes a small window with ECC tasks on multicore systems where setting an Event for a Task on a different core might not be detected immediately.
Modification 5.2.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Spinlocks can be configured with a LockMethod, based on the AUTOSAR 4.1.1 proposed specification and with extra options.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Spinlocks can record usage statistics at run time. See the GetSpinlockInfo API.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The option -{}-debug:build_info may be available, depending on your license. This causes a .bat file to be emitted that shows the build steps that RTA-OS uses.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.2.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: RTAOSCfg emits Alarm Actions within ECUC-CHOICE-CONTAINER-DEF rater than ECUC-PARAM-CONF-CONTAINER-DEF

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: When using hardware counters and starting alarms or schedule tables relative to the current time, it was sometimes possible for to skip the next counter interrupt if it was already due earlier than the interval being requested in this call.
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Use software counters or absolute start times.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.1.3/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added optional callbacks Os_Cbk_TaskStart and Os_Cbk_TaskEnd. These act like Pre/Post Task hooks but don't get called when the task is pre-empted.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.1.3/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added optional callbacks Os_Cbk_ISRStart and Os_Cbk_ISREnd hooks. These get called when an Category 2 ISR starts/ends. They don't get called when the ISR is pre-empted.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.1.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fixes issue introduced in 5.1.1 where a system with untrusted functions, but no untrusted tasks could make Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess for the wrong element when leaving CallTrustedFunction()

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.1.2/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Multicore configurations only: fix issue where TASK execution time monitoring could record incorrect values if preempted by a cross-core ISR.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.1.2/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added 2 new macros: OS_ISRTYPE_TO_INDEX and OS_TASKTYPE_TO_INDEX. These convert an ISRType or TaskType to an index value in the range 0 to the number of ISRs/Tasks.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.1.1/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Support BAS licensing scheme.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.1.1/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Delayed task execution APIs have run-time checks for validity.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.1.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Release.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.95/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fixes issue in GUI where deleting a TASK could result in deletion of OS Applications

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.95/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fixes issue in GUI where window could start on a display that was no longer connected

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.95/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fixes issue in GUI where -{}-env did not affect the build path

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.95/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fixes issue in GUI where reloading a project would result in target configuration information being 'greyed out'

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.95/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fixes issue in GUI where relative paths are specified for include files. Such paths are relative to the project base directory.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.95/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Revised wording in GUI for Expiry Point advance/retard to match the AUTOSAR 4.0.3 form

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.95/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Partial support in GUI for OsAppEcucPartitionRef. The full path has to be entered manually. No checking is done.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.94/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Caters for the changed IOC configuration format for AUTOSAR 4.0.3

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.94/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Expands the documentation and examples around memory protection and Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.93/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Support for AUTOSAR 3.2.2

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.93/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Slight update to AUTOSAR references in VSMD files

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.93/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Delayed Task Execution APIs

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.93/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: 'Only Terminate Untrusted Applications' optimization

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.92/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Supports 4.0.2 and 4.0.3 changes in XML (IOC / ScheduleTables)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.92/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Supports nested packages in the XML

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.91/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The VSMD files have been regenerated for each AUTOSAR version, using the ETAS_RTAOS root identifier

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.91/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Generated XML files reference ETAS_RTAOS rather than /AUTOSAR, /AUTOSAR/EcucDefs or /RTAOS/EcucDefs

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.91/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Generated XML files VALUE-REFs corrected (AUTOSAR 4)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.91/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Generated XML files boolean values are emitted as NUMERICAL values (AUTOSAR 4)

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.90/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Fixes a possible race condition in multicore versions of WaitEvent

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.90/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The code in WaitEvent that checks for OS_ECC_WAIT error was testing the task's worst-case stack usage rather than the actual usage at the point of the call. This could make the stack allocation needed overly pessimistic.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.90/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The algorithm used for license checking has been modified to reduce the number of requests to the ETAS License Manager - particularly for licenses that are *usually* not found.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 5.0.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Release.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.94.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Initial cleanup and optimizations, specifically to reduce the number of calls to get core id in multicore applications.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.93.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Functionally complete AUTOSAR 4.0 and multicore. Not yet optimized.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.93.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Ensure that calls to Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess are done with interrupts locked.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.3/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Extended tasks work with multiple cores

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.3/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: RES_SCHEDULER works across multiple cores

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Register sets work with multiple cores. A register set can include tasks and ISRs on different cores. The OS will use independent save areas for each core. Note that register sets cannot be used to protect data that is shared across cores - you will still need to use spinlocks for this.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.2/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Register sets preserve state across an ECC task WaitEvent call.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.2/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Experimental support for lightweight tasks in multicore if SC1 is selected.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.2/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Increased test coverage on single-core code.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.2/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Significant refactoring of PC-lint/MISRA dismissals (work in progress). PC-lint version 9 is now being used.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.1/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Early access development release for RTA-OS5.0.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.1/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Refinements to target sample applications for multicore.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.1/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Minor change in GUI for the number of cores offered for assignment to OS applications.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.1/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Show OS Size Info report if selected after a build.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.1/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Show license manager on Help menu (if installed).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.1/6
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Remove restriction on max period for Schedule Table Assistant.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.92.1/7
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Updated splash/about screens.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.91.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Early access development release for RTA-OS5.0.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.91.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Preliminary support for AUTOSAR MultiCore OS, including: Starting cores, StartOS, BCC Tasks and ISRs running on different cores, Spinlocks, Resources, Reports. MultiCore elements NOT necessarily supported: ECC tasks, IOC, forced termination, RegisterSets, Trusted functions, Shutdown OS.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.2.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Ensure that calls to Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess are done with interrupts locked.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.2.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Beta release of RTA-OS 4.2. FunctionParams field added to structure passed to Os_Cbk_SetMemoryAccess. It gets used when calling an untrusted function.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.1.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Release of RTA-OS 4.1. No changes post 4.0.91.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.91/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Beta release of RTA-OS 4.1. GUI handling of sample applications fixed (SERN 20110125-DJL1). GUI code generation switch from EXTENDED to STANDARD status fixed (SERN 20101229-DJL2).

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.90/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: First Beta release of RTA-OS 4.1

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Build size report in GUI
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If a non-default output location for .html files was specified, post build reports would not open in the browser, though they would be created correctly.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Pessimistic stack size calculation for ISRs with the same priority
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: With ISRs at the same priority, the maximum stack calculation was pessimistic. It assumed that all ISRs could preempt each other. This only showed on systems where Schedule() was allowed.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.3/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: IOC single-core

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Tracing with compact IDs and extended time
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: When tracing with compact ids and extended time, certain targets would read the stopwatch twice per record (once for each 16 bits). Where the 2 reads straddled a 0x1_0000 boundary, the time recorded appeared to jump backwards.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Large trace buffers and serial links
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Serial links are restricted to 64K frames by RTA-TRACE. The RTA-OS code was trying to limit transmission to 64K, but not including follow-on frames where the buffer size was larger than the frame. It could also mis-calculate the size and crash RTA-TRACE Server.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.2/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: For each build, the tool will generate a log file projectname.log that contains a copy of the text that the tool and compiler output to the screen.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.2/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A batch file named projectname_prebuild.bat can be used to run user-specified code just before the start of a build.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.2/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A batch file named projectname_postbuild.bat can be used to run user-specified code just after the end of a build.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.2/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Where the command-line options - -debug:source and - -nobuild are used together, the source code will be generated without the tool requiring a compiler to be available.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.2/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: The GUI can be directed to store only relative paths in the .rtaos file.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: SetEvent
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If 2 events were set for a waiting ECC task before it was able to resume, then the task could appear to be reactivated twice. This only happened on systems where any task had multiple activations, or where tasks shared a priority.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Register sets
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If an ISR was saving or restoring a register set and a higher prio isr fired then the higher prio isr could save over the same set that the lower one was using. Only affected some of the simpler configurations. Would not occur with stack monitoring, time monitoring or memory protection present.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.1/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: AUTOSAR 4.0 API is supported in addition to AUTOSAR 3.x. The API selected depends upon the AUTOSAR XML version used to configure the project. AUTOSAR 4.0 tightens some previously unspecified behaviors and adds state to OS-Applications. TerminateApplication gains the ability to terminate a specified OS-Application.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.1/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Hooks and callbacks are declared using FUNC(void, OS_CALLOUT_CODE) Os_Cbk_... rather than FUNC(void, OS_APPL_CODE) Os_Cbk_... to be compatible with AUTOSAR 4.0. For compatibility, OS_CALLOUT_CODE is defined as OS_APPL_CODE unless it already exists in Os_Compiler_Cfg.h

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.0/1
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Support for AUTOSAR 4.0 XML, but not the 4.0 API changes. These are planned for the next release.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.0/2
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Split Configuration is supported in rtaoscfg.exe.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.0/3
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Function pointers can be created to most of the OS APIs. Not StartOS/TerminateTask/ChainTask.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.0/4
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Added API GetTaskActivationTime()

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Modification 4.0.0/5
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: Moved default installation location to c:/etas/rta-os

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: NextScheduleTable
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: A repeating ScheduleTable X, that had been 'nexted' to ScheduleTable Y and then 'nexted' from Y would run once and then try to 'next' to Y rather than repeat X.

RELEASE-NOTE.TITLE: Terminating Applications
RELEASE-NOTE.DESCRIPTION: If a task activated by an ISR faulted and the protection hook was told to kill the OS Application, a null reference could occur in the interrupt wrapper.
